events.title: Trump announces 20-point Gaza peace plan
events.detail: Concept of Board of Peace introduced alongside Netanyahu. Sets framework for ceasefire and reconstruction.

events.title: Gaza ceasefire takes effect
events.detail: Peace plan signed by Israel and Hamas on Oct 9. Ceasefire begins Oct 10.

events.title: UN Security Council adopts Resolution 2803
events.detail: Passed 13-0 (Russia and China abstaining). Endorses BoP, authorizes International Stabilization Force, sets mandate through Dec 31, 2027.

events.title: World Bank establishes GRAD Fund
events.detail: Gaza Reconstruction and Development Fund created as Financial Intermediary Fund. World Bank serves as 'limited trustee' with no fiduciary responsibility after funds are transferred to BoP.

events.title: Executive Order 14375 signed
events.detail: Designates BoP as 'public international organization' under IOIA. Grants immunity from lawsuits, inviolable archives, tax exemptions. Legal scholars question statutory basis.

events.title: France declines invitation — first rejection
events.detail: France states charter 'goes beyond the framework of Gaza.' Trump retaliates with threats of 200% wine tariffs. Germany, Norway, Sweden, Slovenia, Ireland also decline in following days.

events.title: Charter signed at Davos by 20 countries
events.detail: Signed at World Economic Forum. Charter establishes $1B permanent seats, 3-year terms, Trump as lifetime chairman. Canada's invitation rescinded after PM Carney criticizes structure.

events.title: Trump pledges $10 billion (U.S. taxpayer)
events.detail: Announced at inaugural BoP meeting. No congressional approval requested, no funding source identified. White House referred to Trump's own remarks when asked for details.

events.title: UAE pledges $1.2B + $1.5B for 'New Rafah'
events.detail: Largest single national pledge. $1.2B described as additional to ~$3B already provided since Oct 2023. Separate $1.5B commitment for 'New Rafah' construction.

events.title: Saudi Arabia pledges $1B (multi-year)
events.detail: Pledged 'over the next few years' — multi-year disbursement, not lump sum.

events.title: Qatar pledges $1B
events.detail: Pledged in full by PM Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani at inaugural meeting.

events.title: Kuwait pledges $1B (multi-year)
events.detail: 'Over the coming years' — multi-year commitment similar to Saudi Arabia.

events.title: 5 additional countries in $7B total
events.detail: Kazakhstan, Azerbaijan, Morocco, Bahrain, Uzbekistan included in $7B announcement but individual amounts undisclosed. Azerbaijan's presidential assistant publicly disputed inclusion.

events.title: JPMorgan in talks to bank for BoP
events.detail: Negotiations for private account accessible by chairman and designates. Conflict of interest: Trump simultaneously has $5B personal lawsuit against JPMorgan and CEO Jamie Dimon.

events.title: Sen. Markey demands oversight answers
events.detail: Letter to Sec. Rubio calls BoP a 'blatant power grab.' Demands answers on legal authority, congressional role, conflicts of interest by March 6. No public response as of March 29.

events.title: BoP opens separate operational account
events.detail: Operational bank account funded by regional actors, separate from the World Bank GRAD Fund. Initial budget for BoP administrative operations.

events.title: UN OCHA $2B fundraising target announced
events.detail: Announced by Trump at inaugural meeting. OCHA would separately solicit from member states — this is a fundraising goal, not a direct pledge of existing funds.

events.title: FIFA pledges $75M partnership
events.detail: Four-phase plan: 50 mini-pitches, 5 full-size pitches, FIFA Academy, 20,000-seat stadium. Strategic partnership agreement signed with FIFA President Infantino.

events.title: Carnegie publishes major fund structure analysis
events.detail: 'On Whose Account?' warns BoP 'will likely devolve into Trump’s private enterprise' when UNSC mandate expires Dec 2027. Details World Bank limited trustee risks, JPMorgan conflicts, lack of safeguards.

events.title: State Dept transfers $1.25B to BoP
events.detail: $1B from International Disaster Assistance, $200M from Peacekeeping Operations, $50M from International Organizations. Executed without congressional consultation during partial government shutdown.

events.title: Sen. Cortez Masto introduces American Homes First Act
events.detail: Would redirect $1B from BoP to LIHEAP. LIHEAP served ~6M households in FY2024. Cortez Masto: 'no transparency about how it is investing its money.'
